entries.title: `/GENPROFILE`, `/FASTGENPROFILE` (Generate Profiling Instrumented Build)
entries.summary: Specifies generation of a *`.pgd`* file by the linker to support profile-guided optimization (PGO). **`/GENPROFILE`** and **`/FASTGENPROFILE`** use different default parameters. Use **`/GENPROFILE`**

entries.title: /GF (Eliminate Duplicate Strings)
entries.summary: Enables the compiler to create a single copy of identical strings in the program image and in memory during execution. This is an optimization called *string pooling* that can create smaller programs.

entries.title: /Gh (Enable _penter hook function)
entries.summary: Causes a call to the `_penter` function at the start of every method or function.

entries.title: /GH (Enable _pexit hook function)
entries.summary: Calls the `_pexit` function at the end of every method or function.

entries.title: `/GL` (Whole program optimization)
entries.summary: Enables whole program optimization.

entries.title: /Gm (Enable Minimal Rebuild)
entries.summary: Deprecated in Visual Studio 2019 version 16.0. Enables minimal rebuild, which determines whether C++ source files that include changed C++ class definitions (stored in header (.h) files) need to be re

entries.title: /GR (Enable Run-Time Type Information)
entries.summary: Adds code to check object types at run time.

entries.title: /GS (Buffer Security Check)
entries.summary: Detects some buffer overruns that overwrite a function's return address, exception handler address, or certain types of parameters. Causing a buffer overrun is a technique used by hackers to exploit c

entries.title: `/Gs` (Control stack checking calls)
entries.summary: Controls the threshold for stack probes.

entries.title: `/GT` (Support fiber-safe thread-local storage)
entries.summary: Supports fiber safety for data allocated using static thread-local storage, that is, data allocated with `__declspec(thread)`.

entries.title: `/guard` (Enable Control Flow Guard)
entries.summary: Enable compiler generation of Control Flow Guard security checks.

entries.title: `/guard:ehcont` (Enable EH Continuation Metadata)
entries.summary: Enables generation of EH Continuation (EHCONT) metadata by the compiler.

entries.title: `/GUARD` (Enable Guard Checks)
entries.summary: Tells the linker whether to support Control Flow Guard checks in the executable image.

entries.title: /Gw (Optimize Global Data)
entries.summary: Package global data in COMDAT sections for optimization.

entries.title: /GX (Enable Exception Handling)
entries.summary: Deprecated. Enables synchronous exception handling using the assumption that functions declared by using `extern "C"` never throw an exception.

entries.title: /Gy (Enable Function-Level Linking)
entries.summary: Allows the compiler to package individual functions in the form of packaged functions (COMDATs).

entries.title: /GZ (Enable Stack Frame Run-Time Error Checking)
entries.summary: Performs the same operations as the [/RTC (Run-Time Error Checks)](rtc-run-time-error-checks.md) option. Deprecated.

entries.title: /H (Restrict Length of External Names)
entries.summary: Deprecated. Restricts the length of external names.

entries.summary: Sets the size of the heap in bytes. This option only applies to executable files.

entries.summary: Exposes the same functionality as the [/HEAP](heap-set-heap-size.md) linker option.

entries.summary: Displays a listing of compiler options to standard output.

entries.summary: Forces parameters passed in registers to also be written to their locations on the stack upon function entry.

entries.title: /jumptablerdata (put switch case jump tables in `.rdata`)
entries.summary: Puts the generated switch case jump tables in the `.rdata` section instead of alongside code in the `.text` section.

entries.title: `/O` options (Optimize code)
entries.summary: The **`/O`** options control various optimizations that help you create code for maximum speed or minimum size.

entries.title: `/O1`, `/O2` (Minimize Size, Maximize Speed)
entries.summary: Selects a predefined set of options that affect the size and speed of generated code.

entries.title: /Ob (Inline Function Expansion)
entries.summary: Controls inline expansion of functions. By default, when optimizing, expansion occurs at the compiler's discretion on all functions, often referred to as *auto-inlining*.

entries.title: /Od (Disable (Debug))
entries.summary: Turns off all optimizations in the program and speeds compilation.

entries.title: `/Og` (Global Optimizations)

entries.title: /Oi (Generate Intrinsic Functions)
entries.summary: Replaces some function calls with intrinsic or otherwise special forms of the function that help your application run faster.

entries.title: `/openmp` (Enable OpenMP Support)
entries.summary: Causes the compiler to process [`#pragma omp`](../../preprocessor/omp.md) directives in support of OpenMP.

entries.title: /OPT (Optimizations)
entries.summary: Controls the optimizations that LINK performs during a build.

entries.title: `/options:strict` (Unrecognized compiler options are errors)
entries.summary: The **`/options:strict`** compiler option tells the compiler to return an error code if a compiler option isn't recognized.

entries.summary: Specifies a key or a key pair to sign a Windows Runtime Metadata (.winmd) file.

entries.summary: Specifies whether to treat linker warnings as errors.

entries.title: /Y- (Ignore Precompiled Header Options)
entries.summary: Causes all other `/Y` compiler options to be ignored (and cannot itself be overridden).

entries.title: /Y (precompiled headers)
entries.summary: The following compiler options affect the generation and use of precompiled headers:

entries.title: /Yc (Create Precompiled Header File)
entries.summary: Instructs the compiler to create a precompiled header (.pch) file that represents the state of compilation at a certain point.

entries.title: /Yd (Place Debug Information in Object File)
entries.summary: Paces complete debugging information in all object files created from a precompiled header (.pch) file when used with the [/Yc](yc-create-precompiled-header-file.md) and [/Z7](z7-zi-zi-debug-informati

entries.title: /Yl (Inject PCH Reference for Debug Library)
entries.summary: The **/Yl** option generates a unique symbol in a precompiled header file, and a reference to this symbol is injected in all object files that use the precompiled header.

entries.title: `/Yu` (Use precompiled header file)
entries.summary: Instructs the compiler to use an existing precompiled header (*`.pch`*) file in the current compilation.

entries.title: `/Z7`, `/Zi`, `/ZI` (Debug Information Format)
entries.summary: The **`/Z7`**, **`/Zi`**, and **`/ZI`** compiler options specify the type of debugging information created for your program, and whether this information is kept in object files or in a program databa

entries.title: /Za, /Ze (Disable Language Extensions)
entries.summary: The **/Za** compiler option disables and emits errors for Microsoft extensions to C that aren't compatible with ANSI C89/ISO C90. The deprecated **/Ze** compiler option enables Microsoft extensions. M

entries.title: `/Zc:alignedNew` (C++17 over-aligned allocation)
entries.summary: Enable support for C++17 over-aligned **`new`**, dynamic memory allocation aligned on boundaries greater than the default for the maximum-sized standard aligned type, `max_align_t`.

entries.title: `/Zc:auto` (Deduce Variable Type)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:auto`** compiler option tells the compiler how to use the [`auto` keyword](../../cpp/auto-cpp.md) to declare variables. If you specify the default option, **`/Zc:auto`**, the compiler deduc

entries.title: `/Zc:char8_t` (Enable C++20 char8_t type)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:char8_t`** compiler option enables C++20 conforming **`char8_t`** type support. **`char8_t`** is a character type that's used to represent UTF-8 code units.

entries.title: `/Zc:checkGwOdr` (Enforce Standard C++ ODR violations under `/Gw`)
entries.summary: This switch enforces C++ standards conformance when using [`/Gw` (Optimize global data)](gw-optimize-global-data.md). When using `/Gw`, certain One Definition Rule (ODR) violations are ignored. This f

entries.title: `/Zc` (Conformance)
entries.summary: Use the **`/Zc`** compiler options to specify standard or Microsoft-specific compiler behavior.

entries.title: `/Zc:__cplusplus` (Enable updated `__cplusplus` macro)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:__cplusplus`** compiler option enables the `__cplusplus` preprocessor macro to report an updated value for recent C++ language standards support. By default, Visual Studio always returns th

entries.title: `/Zc:enumTypes` (Enable enum type deduction)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:enumTypes`** compiler option enables C++ conforming **`enum`** underlying type and enumerator type deduction.

entries.title: `/Zc:externC` (Use Standard C++ `extern "C"` rules)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:externC`** compiler option tells the compiler to conform to the C++ standard and enforce consistent parameter declarations for functions declared as `extern "C"`.

entries.title: `/Zc:externConstexpr` (Enable extern constexpr variables)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:externConstexpr`** compiler option tells the compiler to conform to the C++ standard and allow external linkage for **`constexpr`** variables. By default, Visual Studio always gives a **`co

entries.title: /Zc:forScope (Force Conformance in for Loop Scope)
entries.summary: Used to implement standard C++ behavior for [for](../../cpp/for-statement-cpp.md) loops with Microsoft extensions ([/Ze](za-ze-disable-language-extensions.md)).

entries.title: `/Zc:gotoScope` (Enforce conformance in goto scope)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:gotoScope`** compiler option enables checks for Standard C++ behavior around **`goto`** statements that jump over the initialization of local variables.

entries.title: `/Zc:hiddenFriend` (Enforce Standard C++ hidden friend rules)
entries.summary: Specifies the compiler conforms to the C++ standard treatment of hidden friend functions or function templates.

entries.title: /Zc:implicitNoexcept (Implicit Exception Specifiers)

entries.title: `/Zc:inline` (Remove unreferenced COMDAT)

entries.title: `/Zc:lambda` (Enable updated lambda processor)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:lambda`** compiler option enables conforming lambda grammar and processing support.

entries.title: `/Zc:noexceptTypes` (C++17 noexcept rules)

entries.title: `/Zc:nrvo` (Control optional NRVO)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:nrvo`** compiler option controls Standard C++ optional named return value optimization (NRVO) copy or move elision behavior.

entries.title: `/Zc:preprocessor` (Enable preprocessor conformance mode)
entries.summary: This option enables a token-based preprocessor that conforms to C99 and C++11 and later standards. For more information, see [MSVC new preprocessor overview](../../preprocessor/preprocessor-experiment

entries.title: /Zc:referenceBinding (Enforce reference binding rules)
entries.summary: When the **/Zc:referenceBinding** option is specified, the compiler doesn't allow a non-const lvalue reference to bind to a temporary.

entries.title: /Zc:rvalueCast (Enforce type conversion rules)
entries.summary: When the **`/Zc:rvalueCast`** option is specified, the compiler correctly identifies an rvalue reference type as the result of a cast operation. Its behavior conforms to the C++11 standard. When the o

entries.title: /Zc:sizedDealloc (Enable Global Sized Deallocation Functions)
entries.summary: The **/Zc:sizedDealloc** compiler option tells the compiler to preferentially call global `operator delete` or `operator delete[]` functions that have a second parameter of type `size_t` when the size

entries.title: `/Zc:static_assert` (Strict static_assert handling)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:static_assert`** compiler option tells the compiler to evaluate `static_assert` calls with non-dependent test expressions when class or function templates are parsed.

entries.title: `/Zc:__STDC__` (Enable `__STDC__` macro)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:__STDC__`** compiler option defines the built-in `__STDC__` preprocessor macro as 1 in C code.

entries.title: `/Zc:strictStrings` (Disable string literal type conversion)
entries.summary: When specified, the compiler requires strict **`const`**-qualification conformance for pointers initialized by using string literals.

entries.title: `/Zc:templateScope` (Check template parameter shadowing)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:templateScope`** compiler option enables checks for Standard C++ behavior around shadowing of template parameters.

entries.title: `/Zc:ternary` (Enforce conditional operator rules)
entries.summary: Enable enforcement of C++ Standard rules for the types and const or volatile (cv) qualification of the second and third operands in a conditional operator expression.

entries.title: /Zc:threadSafeInit (Thread-safe Local Static Initialization)

entries.title: /Zc:throwingNew (Assume operator new throws)

entries.title: `/Zc:tlsGuards` (Check TLS initialization)
entries.summary: The **`/Zc:tlsGuards`** compiler option generates runtime checks for thread local storage (TLS) initialization in DLLs.

entries.title: `/Zc:trigraphs` (Trigraphs Substitution)

entries.title: /Zl (Omit Default Library Name)

entries.title: `/Zm` (Specify precompiled header memory allocation limit)

entries.title: /Zo (Enhance Optimized Debugging)
entries.summary: Generate enhanced debugging information for optimized code in non-debug builds.

entries.title: /Zp (Struct Member Alignment)
entries.summary: Controls how the members of a structure are packed into memory and specifies the same packing for all structures in a module.

entries.title: /Zs (Syntax Check Only)
entries.summary: Tells the compiler to check only the syntax of the source files on the command line.

entries.title: `/ZW` (Windows Runtime Compilation)
entries.summary: Compiles source code to support Microsoft C++ component extensions C++/CX for the creation of Universal Windows Platform (UWP) apps.
